# Helmfile Bundle

Generated by AICR v1.0.0

## Prerequisites

- `helmfile` CLI on `$PATH` ([installation guide](https://helmfile.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#installation))
- `helm` CLI on `$PATH` (helmfile invokes helm under the hood)
- `helm-diff` plugin: `helm plugin install https://github.com/databus23/helm-diff`
  (helmfile's `apply` and `diff` subcommands invoke it under the hood)
- `kubectl` configured for your target cluster

## Components

| Release | Namespace | Version |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| cert-manager | cert-manager | v1.17.2 |
| gpu-operator | gpu-operator | v25.3.3 |

## Deployment

Run all `helmfile` commands from this directory so it picks up `helmfile.yaml`
by default. The release graph (`needs:` dependencies) lives in
`helmfile.yaml` — helmfile handles ordering, parallelism, and idempotent
re-apply. For bundles whose dependency DAG
spans more than one level, `helmfile.yaml` is a thin orchestration
document with a `helmfiles:` list: one sub-helmfile per level
(`level-0.yaml` first, `level-1.yaml` next, …). `helmfile` runs the
sub-helmfiles sequentially, so by the time level K is processed every
release in levels 0…K-1 has fully applied — `helmfile diff` against a
fresh cluster no longer fails to resolve CRDs (or any other resources)
installed by an earlier level before a later level references them
(see issue #914).


```bash
# Preview changes (no cluster mutation):
helmfile diff

# Install or upgrade everything in dependency order:
helmfile apply

# Tear everything down in reverse dependency order:
helmfile destroy
```

## Troubleshooting

- Validate the document without applying: `helmfile lint`
- See exactly which helm commands helmfile will run: `helmfile --debug apply`
- Apply only a subset by name: `helmfile -l name=<release> apply`
- Inspect a specific release after apply: `helm status <release> -n <namespace>`

For deployer scope and the rationale behind helmfile vs the bash deployer,
see issue [#632](https://github.com/NVIDIA/aicr/issues/632).
